evals.prompt: A UNRA trunk road project in northern Uganda involves rehabilitation of 65 km of national road. The estimated contract value is UGX 80 billion (approximately USD 21 million). The project is funded by World Bank IDA Credit. The project has an agreed Procurement Plan with the World Bank. There are both domestic and international contractors with relevant experience. Advise on the correct procurement method, prior review requirements, and contract form.
evals.expected_output: World Bank procurement rules apply (not PPDA thresholds alone). ICB selected because USD 21M exceeds the USD 15M ICB threshold. Prior review required. World Bank no-objection needed for bidding documents, evaluation report, and award. FIDIC Red Book contract form (Employer provides design). Prequalification recommended for a contract of this size.

assertions.text: Identifies World Bank as the applicable procurement framework (not PPDA alone) based on funding source
Selects International Competitive Bidding (ICB) based on value exceeding USD 15 million threshold
States that prior review is required for this contract value
Identifies World Bank no-objection requirements at key stages (bidding documents, evaluation, award)
Recommends FIDIC Red Book (Skill #42) as the contract form for traditional construction

evals.prompt: A district local government in central Uganda plans to upgrade a 15 km district road from gravel to paved standard. The estimated contract value is UGX 3 billion. The project is funded entirely from the Government of Uganda budget (no donor funding). There are at least 6 domestic contractors with experience in similar district road projects. No urgency or special circumstances apply. Advise on the correct procurement method, approval requirements, and standard bidding documents to use.
evals.expected_output: PPDA Act framework applies (GoU funded). Open Domestic Bidding selected because UGX 3 billion exceeds the UGX 500 million threshold from Guideline 1/2014. Contracts Committee approval required for method selection, bidding documents, evaluation report, and award. PPDA Standard Bidding Documents for works. No prior review from donors (GoU funded).

assertions.text: Applies PPDA Act framework based on GoU funding source identification
Selects Open Domestic Bidding based on UGX 3 billion exceeding UGX 500 million threshold
Identifies Contracts Committee as the approval authority
Specifies PPDA Standard Bidding Documents (not World Bank SBDs)
Flags thresholds as illustrative with recommendation to verify current values from PPDA Authority

evals.prompt: Following severe flooding in eastern Uganda, a major culvert on a national road has been completely washed out, cutting off access to several communities. UNRA needs to replace the culvert urgently. The estimated cost is UGX 150 million, funded from GoU emergency budget. Only 2 local contractors can mobilise within the required timeframe. The flooding event occurred 4 days ago and was unforeseeable. Advise on procurement method options, justification requirements, and any limitations on the use of emergency provisions.
evals.expected_output: Emergency provisions under PPDA Regulation 8 are applicable (urgent, unforeseeable, not caused by PDE delays). However, even in emergencies, competition must be maximised. Value falls in quotation method range (UGX 10M-200M). Recommended: quotation method with shortened timeline rather than direct procurement. If only 1 contractor can mobilise, direct procurement with comprehensive justification. Contracts Committee approval still required. Document emergency circumstances fully.

assertions.text: Considers emergency/direct procurement under PPDA provisions but notes limitations
Notes that even in emergencies, competitive methods should be considered first (Regulation 8(5))
Identifies comprehensive justification requirements for emergency procurement
Recognises that with 2 available contractors, a competitive method (quotation with modifications) is feasible
States Contracts Committee approval is still required for emergency procurement

evals.prompt: KeNHA needs to procure a road rehabilitation project with an estimated cost of KES 800 million. The project is funded by the Government of Kenya (GoK). Multiple domestic contractors are available with experience in similar projects. No urgency or special circumstances apply. Advise on the appropriate procurement method under Kenya PPRA regulations.
evals.expected_output: PPRA Act 2015 framework applies (GoK funded). Open National Tendering is the appropriate method -- KES 800 million exceeds the PPRA threshold for Open Tendering. Open Tendering is the default method under Section 91. Prequalification may be considered given the contract size. PPRA Standard Bidding Documents for Works should be used. Tender Committee approval required. Contract form: FIDIC Red Book (Skill #42) with KeNHA COPA. All thresholds are illustrative and should be verified against current PPRA regulations.

assertions.text: References PPRA Act 2015 as the applicable procurement framework for GoK-funded project
Recommends Open Tendering (above threshold) as the appropriate procurement method
Mentions PPRA regulatory requirements for procurement method selection
Flags thresholds as illustrative or notes need to verify against current PPRA regulations
